medical_information.what_medications_are_you_currently_takin:
1. Lisinopril 10 mg tablet – once daily in the morning for high blood pressure (started around 2022).
2. Atorvastatin (Lipitor) 20 mg tablet – once daily at night for cholesterol.
3. Metformin 500 mg tablet – twice daily with breakfast and dinner for borderline type 2 diabetes / insulin resistance.
4. Vitamin D3 2000 IU – once daily in the morning (over-the-counter) because previous bloodwork showed low vitamin D.
5. Cetirizine 10 mg – once daily as needed during spring and fall for seasonal allergies (pollen and ragweed).
6. Ibuprofen 200 mg – 1–2 tablets as needed for occasional headaches or muscle aches (no more than a few times per month).
medical_information.please_explain_any_reactions_here:
I have had a mild but noticeable reaction to amoxicillin in the past. About 10 years ago, I was prescribed amoxicillin for a sinus infection. Within a day or two of starting the medication, I developed an itchy, blotchy red rash on both arms and across my chest. There was no trouble breathing, no swelling of the lips or tongue, and no need for emergency care, but my primary care doctor at the time advised me to stop taking amoxicillin immediately and switched me to a different antibiotic (azithromycin), which I tolerated without any issues. Since then, I have always told medical and dental offices that I may have an allergy or sensitivity to amoxicillin and possibly other penicillin-type antibiotics, and I have avoided those medications.

I also tend to get some mild stomach upset (nausea and loose stools) with stronger pain medications like hydrocodone–acetaminophen. After a minor procedure a few years ago, I was prescribed hydrocodone–acetaminophen for pain. I took it for one day and felt very nauseated, lightheaded, and constipated, so I stopped and switched to alternating ibuprofen and acetaminophen instead, which controlled the pain well with no significant side effects. I do not consider this a life-threatening allergy, more of an intolerance, but I prefer to avoid narcotic pain medications when possible.

For dental materials, I have not had any known reactions to local anesthetics, latex, or dental filling materials in the past. I have had multiple fillings and one crown done over the last several years and did not experience any swelling, unusual pain, or rash afterwards. Local anesthetics like lidocaine with epinephrine have worked well for me; I only notice the expected temporary numbness and a little bit of heart racing, which I was told is normal because of the epinephrine.
medical_information.do_you_have_any_other_health_issues_or_a:
Other health issues:
- Mild hypertension: Diagnosed approximately in 2022. Currently controlled with lisinopril 10 mg daily. My typical blood pressure at home runs around 125–135 / 80–85 when I check it a few times a week.
- Borderline type 2 diabetes / insulin resistance: My A1C has been in the 6.0–6.3% range over the last couple of years. I was started on metformin 500 mg twice daily along with diet and exercise changes. I have not had any diabetic complications (no neuropathy, vision changes, or kidney issues that I am aware of).
- High cholesterol (hyperlipidemia): Diagnosed a few years ago, currently treated with atorvastatin 20 mg daily. My last lipid panel earlier this year was improved but still being monitored.
- Seasonal/environmental allergies: Mostly in the spring and fall (pollen, ragweed). Symptoms include sneezing, itchy eyes, and sinus congestion. I manage this with cetirizine and occasional nasal saline rinses.
- Occasional tension headaches and neck/shoulder muscle tightness, usually related to stress and long hours at a computer. I treat this with stretching, better posture, and occasional ibuprofen.

Other allergies or sensitivities:
- Suspected amoxicillin/penicillin allergy as described above (itchy rash after taking amoxicillin). I avoid all penicillin-class antibiotics unless specifically cleared by an allergist, which I have not done yet.
- No known food allergies. I tolerate nuts, shellfish, dairy, and gluten without any issues.
- No history of asthma, eczema, or anaphylaxis.

Dental/medical considerations:
- I tend to get a bit anxious before dental procedures, especially anything involving drilling or injections. I do better when the dentist explains what is happening step-by-step and checks in periodically. Numbing usually works well, but I sometimes need an extra dose of local anesthetic if the procedure is long or if it involves lower molars.
- I do not have a history of bleeding disorders, and I have not had any problems with excessive bleeding at the dentist or during past minor medical procedures.
- I do not smoke and only drink alcohol socially (a couple of times per month at most).

If there are any concerns about antibiotic choices for dental work, I would prefer to avoid amoxicillin and other penicillin-based medications unless there is no alternative and the risk is carefully evaluated.
